element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Solo combat against early slimes and small monsters

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game (recruitment of Prince of Cannock and Princess of Moonbrooke)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Learning to coordinate multiple heroes in battle formations
Strategic ordering of party actions against multiple foes (upgrade from Dragon Quest I's one-on-one
Gradual acquisition as characters level; buffing, healing, offensive magic
Purchasing weapons and armor with gold earned from battles
Opens non-linear exploration; enables access to distant regions
Navigating safe floors vs. hazardous paths; floor patterns indicate safety (cross-shaped = safe in JP version, square in US version)
Final confrontation with wizard Hargon requires well-leveled, equipped party

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully defeating a monster group and gaining experience
Learning new spells that expand tactical options
typical_tension_triggers: Low HP/MP conditions requiring resource management
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual difficulty escalation with mid-game plateau; known for late-game difficulty spike requiring extensive grinding and strategic party composition

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Ship access enables exploration beyond initial linear path; non-linear progression available after obtaining boat
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Safe floor design uses visual patterns: cross-shaped safe zones (JP) vs. square with directional indicators (US localization)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with solo hero and weak slimes; early enemy stats adjusted downward in US version to ease progression
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Scaling challenge as multiple party members join; introduction of multi-enemy formations requiring tactical coordination
difficulty_curve.late_game: Steep difficulty spike notorious across all versions; Hargon Castle serves as extreme challenge requiring extensive leveling and perfect party composition
notable_spikes: Boss encounters in Moonbrooke Castle destruction sequence
Hargon's Castle final dungeon and wizard Hargon boss fight
Late-game enemy stat values significantly higher than mid-game

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear early progression with safe zones
notable_level_patterns.description: Initial towns and dungeons gated by monster difficulty; each location represents clear progression checkpoint with save opportunities via king/minister NPCs
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Starting towns and initial dungeons before ship acquisition

notable_level_patterns.description: After obtaining ship, player can access multiple regions in flexible order; exploration becomes key gameplay component
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Post-ship world with multiple accessible castle/dungeon destinations

notable_level_patterns.description: Multi-floor dungeon with hazardous and safe passages; floor design communicates safety via visual pattern (safe cross-shaped floor in JP vs. marked square in US); requires repeated Stepguard spell use in US version due to redesigned layout
